value_picks.name: Accenture plc

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $12.16B / mcap $119.39B = 10.19% FCF yield, well above 7% pass bar.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 13.01 vs implied PE at target median 250/14.909 = 16.77; discount ~22.4% falls in 15-30% borderline band.

drawdown.detail: Down 40.44% from 52w high $325.71 — exceeds 35% pass bar.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.06B (cash $9.41B vs debt $8.35B); D/E 25.47 and CR 1.336 both in pass range.

dividend.detail: Yield 3.36% with payout ratio 51.0% — both inside pass thresholds.

institutional.detail: Institutional 82.96%, recommendation_mean 1.86; 18 buys vs 10 holds out of 28.

ai_exposure.detail: Consulting book benefits from AI-transformation deals (bookings tailwind), but the labor-hour services model is structurally threatened if clients substitute GenAI copilots for billable staff — net-neutral until mix shifts.

deal_radar.assessment: Blue-chip services leader trading at a bookings-cycle low; risk/reward skewed positive with capital-return cushion.

catalysts_general: Forward PE 13.0 is ~22% below the analyst-implied target PE of 16.8
Free-cash-flow yield ~10.2% with dividend yield 3.36% supports a total shareholder-yield floor
Price sits ~0.5% below SMA-20 and 5.8% below SMA-50 — stabilization vs down 32.6% in 90 days
Short interest rose 19.7% MoM to 3.32% of float, modest bearish pressure but still low in absolute terms
Net-cash balance sheet ($1.06B) plus ROE 24.8% preserves buyback/dividend capacity

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52-week low $177.50 on another bookings miss
Services recession — consulting demand contracts 10%+ and margins compress
GFC-style de-rating with GenAI substitution fears accelerating

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above the $177.50 52w low, giving a sub-12x forward PE and ~11% FCF yield while avoiding catching a falling knife.

comparables.note: Cheaper IT-services peer in same input set; smaller scale, higher beta
Not in input; larger, slower-growth services peer trades at premium — ACN looks cheaper on growth-adjusted basis

value_picks.analyst_take: Accenture offers the cleanest risk/reward in the cohort: 10% FCF yield, 3.4% dividend, net cash, and 40% drawdown on what remains a #1 global consulting franchise. The AI-substitution fear is real but offset by AI-transformation bookings — a pass on 5.5/6 value criteria warrants a BUY with scaling at $182 and below.

The GAAP-loss optics and SBC dilution are real, but cash generation and analyst conviction argue for a BUY with scaling below $60.
